agent_spec_note: sudd/agents/code-analyzer-be.md prescribes an HTTP-framework schema (fastapi|express|...). This repo has no HTTP backend — it is a Go CLI (cobra) + embedded markdown framework. Output uses the best-effort fallback schema specified by the invoking orchestrator: tech_stack, cli_surface[], internal_packages[], state_json_shape, gaps[], concerns[], summary.

runtime_companion.name: persona-browser-agent
runtime_companion.note: Not present in this repo — auto-installed by `sudd init` when sibling directory is detected.

cli_surface.name: init
cli_surface.has_tests: partial (main_test.go covers helpers; no end-to-end test for runInit)

concerns.evidence: main.go:252 — filepath.Abs error path exits cleanly but target default of `.` is silently used without arg validation.

cli_surface.name: status
reads: state.json via auto.LoadState (main.go:630 — best-effort; silently skips on error, which is intended per comment)

cli_surface.name: update
cli_surface.has_tests: partial (installer livesource_test.go covers the copy engine)
guards: Self-update refusal (main.go:667-679): refuses when target == sudd2 source repo unless --allow-self-update.
Shrink-gate via installer.UpdateOptions.AllowShrink (main.go:762) — refuses writes that shrink >50% or to 0 bytes.

cli_surface.name: auto
state_writes: sudd/state.json (auto.SaveState — many call sites; NON-ATOMIC, see concerns)
sudd/changes/active/<id>/ → archive/ | stuck/ | dirty/ via performArchival (runner.go:660)
guards: deferred finalize (auto.go:239) runs MarkSessionEnd on panic/normal/signal/budget exit

cli_surface.name: audit
cli_surface.has_tests: no (no audit_test.go at CLI layer; auto.RunAudit is a thin subprocess wrapper with no unit test either)
cli_surface.delegates_to: auto.RunAudit (internal/auto/audit.go:15) — builds /sudd-audit prompt and launches CLI subprocess.

cli_surface.name: restore-snapshot
state_writes: overwrites sudd/ framework files from snapshot tarball

cli_surface.name: vision
cli_surface.handler: visionCmd (read-only group with `context` child at vision.go:24)

cli_surface_discrepancy.finding: sudd/codebase-manifest.json:92 lists a `fleet` CLI subcommand ("fleet status/update helpers for multi-project rollups") but there is NO cobra command named `fleet` registered. fleet.go contains only helper functions (statusAllSiblingsRun, updateAllSiblingsRun, readSuddVersion, etc.) invoked by the --all-siblings flags on status/update/metrics.
cli_surface_discrepancy.evidence: sudd-go/cmd/sudd/fleet.go has no init() function and no rootCmd.AddCommand call; grep across sudd-go for `fleetCmd` or `"fleet"` as a cobra Use: returns zero matches. Confirmed via `grep -rn fleet sudd-go/cmd/sudd/` — only --all-siblings flag descriptions reference 'fleet status' in prose.

dead_code_candidates.name: AutoSession.UnmarshalJSON legacy-numeric branch
dead_code_candidates.evidence: Comment claims the format was 'legacy numeric format from older orchestrators' — no longer producible by any code path in-tree. Remains defensively to tolerate forward-compat reads, so not strictly dead, but should be documented with a sunset date.

dead_code_candidates.name: RollupSessionStats (unused after inline stats rewrite)
dead_code_candidates.evidence: Comment says 'Called once at session end' but runAutoCmd no longer calls it — stats are now bumped inline via IncrementChangeStats (auto.go:373) and the deferred finalizer (auto.go:592) explicitly no longer rolls up. Function is orphaned but still exported. grep sudd-go for 'RollupSessionStats' outside state.go/state_test.go to confirm.

concerns.title: auto.SaveState writes sudd/state.json non-atomically
concerns.evidence: sudd-go/internal/auto/state.go:178 — `return os.WriteFile(statePath(projectDir), data, 0644)`. No tmp+fsync+rename. Compare with internal/state/write.go:78 writeAtomic and internal/vision/vision.go:86 atomicWrite which DO use the atomic pattern. `sudd auto` calls SaveState ~6-8 times per processed change (queue advance, inline stats, counter bumps, vision-processed-at, etc.) — a crash or SIGKILL mid-write leaves state.json partially written/corrupted. A corrupt state.json breaks the next session's LoadState entirely (fatal error in LoadState at state.go:109). The framework documents 'EnsureGitClean' and fsync-safe markdown writes as design invariants; state.json — the single most load-bearing piece of state — violates that invariant.
concerns.remediation: Replace os.WriteFile with tmp+fsync+rename (copy writeAtomic from internal/state/write.go:78).

concerns.title: SaveState errors in auto.go are logged to stderr but never abort the session
concerns.evidence: sudd-go/cmd/sudd/auto.go lines 186, 282, 377, 394, 410, 423, 464, 545, 603, 615 — every SaveState call uses the pattern `if saveErr := auto.SaveState(...); saveErr != nil { fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: ...", saveErr) }`. If the disk fills or perms break, the whole auto session continues processing changes while state.json is never updated. Combined with the non-atomic write above, this means a corrupted state.json is emitted without any propagation to the operator.
concerns.remediation: At minimum, increment a 'save_failures' counter and abort the loop after N consecutive failures.

concerns.title: RunAudit swallows stderr into audit.log without surfacing meaningful error classification
concerns.evidence: sudd-go/internal/auto/audit.go:63-66 — any subprocess error is wrapped as `audit subprocess: %w` and returned. The exit code (e.g. timeout vs. network failure vs. LLM quota exhaustion) is not mapped to distinct outcomes. The CLI caller (audit.go:97) then just prints 'Audit failed' with the raw error and os.Exits(1) — user has no guidance on whether to retry, escalate, or investigate. The /sudd-audit markdown orchestrator is expected to know how to fail, but Go has no visibility into partial artifacts (e.g. codeintel.json exists but report.md does not).
concerns.remediation: Parse audit.log tail for STUCK/CRITICAL/TIMEOUT markers and surface a classification (audit CLI.go:99).

concerns.title: RunDiscovery assumes opencode and claude use identical prompt syntax
concerns.evidence: sudd-go/internal/auto/discovery.go:66-70 — `prompt := "/sudd-discover --force"; if cliBinary == "opencode" { prompt = "/sudd-discover --force" }` — the two branches are identical. Either the opencode special case is defunct or was never needed. Dead conditional.

tests: (+others for DONE_DIRTY outcome and runner STUCK reason per manifest)
regression_guard.notes: Runs against LIVE repo tree (sudd/agents/*, sudd/standards.md) to catch the v3.8.x self-wipe regression. Uses findSUDDRepoRoot walk-up heuristic (framework_integrity_test.go:30). Correctly t.Skipf when not in repo.

concerns.title: No regression guard for template count drift below live
concerns.evidence: manifest comment block at framework_integrity_test.go:14-26 describes the v3.8.x damage where 'the shipping template in sudd-go/cmd/sudd/templates/ had become stale and was missing 9 agents.' The test asserts live count > 0 but does not compare live sudd/agents/ count to templates/ sudd/agents/ count — so a future silent template drift would still ship.
concerns.remediation: Add TestTemplateCountMatchesLive comparing fs.WalkDir of live sudd/agents/ vs templateFS sudd/agents/.

internal_packages.name: internal/state
internal_packages.notes: Owns sudd/CURRENT_STATE.md writes. Uses tmp+fsync+rename (write.go:78). Snapshots prior file to auto-reports/history/ before overwrite.

internal_packages.name: internal/vision
internal_packages.notes: Owns sudd/vision.md writes. Uses tmp+fsync+rename (vision.go:86). Kill switch SUDD_VISION_PATH=off is read-safe (writes disabled, reads unchanged).

concerns.title: MigrateVisionMD has a dead statement
concerns.evidence: sudd-go/internal/vision/vision.go:289 — `_ = i` is a no-op assignment left inside the titleBlock parse loop. Harmless, but indicates incomplete refactor.

fields_read_from_raw_map: stats.{tasks_completed, tasks_stuck, tasks_blocked, total_retries, total_done_dirty, stats_seeded} (state.go:532-679)
retry_count, tests_passed, gate_passed, personas_validated, architecture_reviewed, design_gate_passed, gate_score, design_gate_score (ClearChangeState @ state.go:191-218)

roundtrip_safety.assessment: GOOD for the typed fields — raw map preserves unknown fields verbatim. LoadState (auto/state.go:102-142) unmarshals into map[string]json.RawMessage first, then pulls typed fields; SaveState (auto/state.go:146-179) marshals typed fields back into the raw map. Pattern is correct.
roundtrip_safety.issue: WRITE IS NOT ATOMIC — see cli_surface → auto concerns. Any crash mid-write produces truncated JSON that LoadState cannot parse on next session (auto/state.go:109 returns fmt.Errorf("parse state.json: %w"); runAutoCmd os.Exit(1)s on that error at auto.go:177-179).

gaps.title: state.json writes are not atomic
gaps.impact: Crash mid-save produces unparseable state.json; next `sudd auto` exits with 'parse state.json' error (auto.go:177). Worse, the morning report never gets written (WriteReport is after the affected save sites). An 8-hour auto run can lose all stats.

gaps.title: `fleet` CLI subcommand advertised in manifest but not registered
gaps.evidence: sudd/codebase-manifest.json:92 vs sudd-go/cmd/sudd/fleet.go has no init() and no rootCmd.AddCommand.
gaps.impact: Documentation/reality drift; anyone reading the manifest and running `sudd fleet status` gets 'unknown command' from cobra. Does not break any workflow, but misleads operators and audit agents.

gaps.title: SaveState errors are warnings, never abort the loop
gaps.impact: Disk full / perms regression / read-only FS would produce stderr warnings nobody reads during an overnight run, while the loop keeps processing changes that can never be persisted.

gaps.title: No end-to-end test for `sudd audit` CLI
gaps.evidence: No sudd-go/cmd/sudd/audit_test.go; internal/auto/audit.go is a thin subprocess launcher with no unit test.
gaps.impact: Changes to prompt formatting (audit.go:16-25) or subprocess env handling could silently break audit runs — only detected when a night's audit fails.

gaps.title: RunAudit does not classify subprocess exit codes
gaps.impact: Timeout vs. quota vs. crash all surface as 'audit subprocess: <err>' — operator can't triage without reading audit.log.

gaps.title: Dead code: RollupSessionStats no longer called by runAutoCmd
gaps.evidence: sudd-go/internal/auto/state.go:467 — docstring says 'Called once at session end', but auto.go:500-507 explicitly documents that stats rollup is no longer done there (inline IncrementChangeStats replaced it).
gaps.impact: Confusing for maintainers; may be re-introduced if someone reads the docstring and rewires the caller.

gaps.title: Discovery prompt has dead conditional
gaps.evidence: sudd-go/internal/auto/discovery.go:66-70 — both branches of the opencode/claude switch produce identical prompt string.
gaps.impact: Harmless but telegraphs unfinished per-CLI prompt divergence work.

gaps.title: No regression guard comparing live agent count to embedded template agent count
gaps.evidence: sudd-go/internal/installer/framework_integrity_test.go asserts live != 0 but doesn't compare to templateFS.
gaps.impact: Exactly the scenario framework_integrity_test.go was written to prevent (template drift below live) would still slip through if tomorrow's regression is 'shipped template shrinks by 5 agents' instead of 'live wipes'.

concerns.title: state.json is written non-atomically and crash-unsafe
concerns.details: `os.WriteFile` with no tmp+rename. Every other markdown-owning write path in the codebase (internal/state/write.go:78 writeAtomic, internal/vision/vision.go:86 atomicWrite) uses tmp+fsync+rename. state.json is the most load-bearing piece of SUDD state and violates that invariant.

concerns.title: Save failures in runAutoCmd are logged but never escalate
concerns.details: Per the user's MEMORY entry ('Preflight blocks by default, never silent-fails'), this is the exact anti-pattern the framework warns against — warn-and-continue on config/state failures. state.json save errors during auto are literally the session's memory; losing them silently is a violation of the 'fail loud' contract.

concerns.title: sudd/codebase-manifest.json lists `fleet` as a cobra command but no such command exists
concerns.evidence_file_line: sudd/codebase-manifest.json:92 vs. sudd-go/cmd/sudd/fleet.go (no init, no AddCommand)
concerns.details: Fleet behavior is accessed only via --all-siblings flags on status/update/metrics. Running `sudd fleet <anything>` fails.

concerns.title: `sudd audit` has no Go-level tests and no subprocess outcome classification
concerns.details: Audit is the second-most-important autonomous surface (after `sudd auto`) and has zero unit-test coverage at either the CLI wrapper or internal helper layer. Any exit from the subprocess is mapped to 'audit subprocess: <err>' with no timeout vs quota vs crash distinction. The CLI caller (audit.go:97) just os.Exits(1) — no actionable guidance to operator.

concerns.title: auto.RollupSessionStats is orphaned but still exported
concerns.details: Docstring claims 'Called once at session end' — but auto.go:500-507 explicitly says rollup is no longer needed because IncrementChangeStats bumps inline. Exported symbol with stale contract invites future re-wiring that would double-count. The pattern of inline stats was introduced specifically to avoid a mid-session-crash data loss; re-adding the rollup would reintroduce the same bug.

summary.headline: SUDD's Go CLI is well-structured and heavily tested at the internal package layer, but three issues materially affect reliability: (1) state.json is written non-atomically, the single riskiest defect given how many times SaveState fires per auto session; (2) state-save failures are swallowed as warnings, violating the framework's own 'fail loud' principle; (3) the `sudd audit` surface has zero unit tests and opaque error classification.
top5_concerns: CRITICAL: internal/auto/state.go:178 — state.json write is not atomic (`os.WriteFile` instead of tmp+fsync+rename). Inconsistent with internal/state/write.go:78 and internal/vision/vision.go:86 which ARE atomic. Any crash mid-write breaks the next `sudd auto` session (LoadState error path → os.Exit(1) at auto.go:177).

HIGH: sudd-go/cmd/sudd/auto.go lines 186, 282, 377, 394, 410, 423, 464, 545, 603, 615 — SaveState errors print 'Warning:' to stderr and continue. An overnight run with a broken disk silently drops every change's state update. Violates the user's documented 'never silent-fail on config failures' invariant (MEMORY/feedback_preflight_block_by_default.md).
MEDIUM: sudd/codebase-manifest.json:92 lists a `fleet` CLI command that does not exist. fleet.go has no init() and no rootCmd.AddCommand — only helpers for --all-siblings flags on other commands.

MEDIUM: sudd-go/internal/auto/audit.go:57-66 — audit subprocess failures are wrapped uniformly (`audit subprocess: %w`) with no exit-code classification (timeout vs quota vs crash). CLI caller at audit.go:97 just os.Exits(1). Zero tests at either audit.go or internal/auto/audit.go.

MEDIUM: sudd-go/internal/auto/state.go:467 — RollupSessionStats is orphaned but still exported. auto.go:500-507 explicitly no longer calls it (inline IncrementChangeStats replaced it, specifically to survive mid-session crashes). Stale docstring ('Called once at session end') invites future re-wiring that would reintroduce the data-loss regression the inline change fixed.
strengths: internal/state/write.go and internal/vision/vision.go both use the correct tmp+fsync+rename atomic-write pattern — proof the team knows how to do this correctly elsewhere.
framework_integrity_test.go (685 lines) provides real regression guards against the v3.8.x self-wipe bug.
Deferred session finalizer in auto.go:239 correctly handles panic, signal, budget, and normal exit paths — a solid invariant.
State struct's raw-map preservation pattern (auto/state.go:82-142, 146-179) preserves unknown fields across load/save cycles.
Inline stats bump (auto.go:373) is the right fix for mid-session crash stat loss; the pattern just needs state.json writes to be atomic to be fully correct.
